items.title: aptos-core aptos-node-v1.41.1-rc
items.body:
## What's Changed
* [Hot State] Enable pruner for hot state merkle db by @wqfish in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18467
* [Network] Add access control policies to network configs. by @JoshLind in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18504
* Enhance log entry serialization by converting structured log values t… by @zekun000 in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18447
* [vm] Fix async code state replay by @georgemitenkov in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18479
* [REST API] Add `chain_id` and `node_type` to the `/info` endpoint by @JoshLind in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18525
* [Storage] Fix for panics in state update code and improve error messages by @wqfish in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18610


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/compare/aptos-node-v1.40.4...aptos-node-v1.41.0-rc
items.body_full:
## What's Changed
* [Hot State] Enable pruner for hot state merkle db by @wqfish in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18467
* [Network] Add access control policies to network configs. by @JoshLind in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18504
* Enhance log entry serialization by converting structured log values t… by @zekun000 in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18447
* [vm] Fix async code state replay by @georgemitenkov in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18479
* [REST API] Add `chain_id` and `node_type` to the `/info` endpoint by @JoshLind in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18525
* [Storage] Fix for panics in state update code and improve error messages by @wqfish in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18610


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/compare/aptos-node-v1.40.4...aptos-node-v1.41.0-rc

items.title: aptos-core aptos-node-v1.40.4
items.body:
This one is opensourcing the aptos-node-v1.40.4-hotfix, if you already upgraded to the hotfix, no need to upgrade again.

## What's Changed
[vm] removes usage of unwrap from cryptographic native natives
[vm] uses full transaction size for gas and checks
[vm] checks closure depth bound at construction time
[consensus] caps the number of consensus threads to 32
[execution] caps the number of BlockSTM execution threads to 16
[quorum store] fixes a bug in batch generator when transaction sizes exceed batch size.


* [cp][aptos-release-v1.40] consensus/execution: perf tuning by @aptos-bot in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18678
* cherry-pick recent hotfixes (1.40) by @vgao1996 in https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18667


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/compare/aptos-node-v1.40.2-hotfix...aptos-node-v1.40.4

items.title: [prover][cleanup] Remove dead options and flags
items.body:
## Description

Cleanup of dead prover options and modernize option parsing:

- Remove dead fields from `Options` (`run_read_write_set`, `skip_attribute_checks`, `model_builder`) and their CLI args/handlers

items.title: [storage] Make is_descendant_of check strict by walking the parent chain
items.body:
The previous implementation only checked that both nodes belonged to
the same family and that `self.layer >= other.layer`. This is
insufficient for forked chains: two nodes on different forks can
satisfy both conditions without one being an ancestor of the other.

Strengthen the check by walking up the parent chain from `self` via
weak-pointer upgrades until we either find `other` (true descendant)
or pass its layer / hit a broken link (not a descendant).

Add a test covering reflexive, direct lineage, cross-fork, and
cross-family cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Small, localized change to an ancestry predicate plus new tests; main risk is behavior change for callers that previously relied on the looser same-family/layer heuristic.
> 
> **Overview**
> Tightens `MapLayer::is_descendant_of` to verify true ancestry by walking `self`’s parent chain (via `Weak::upgrade`) instead of relying on same-family + layer index comparisons, preventing false positives across forks and handling dropped parents safely.
> 
> Adds a focused unit test building a forked layer tree to assert reflexive, direct-lineage, cross-fork, and cross-family descendant behavior.
>

This change improves type system validation and fixes several issues in async runtime type checks to prevent divergence between execution and replay.

**Changes:**
- Add depth tracking to type node counting during gas charging
- Fix type argument substitution for captured parameters in interpreter
- Improve branch instruction consistency in async replay
- Add VMConfig flag for depth checking gated by gas version >= 1.41
- Fix documentation and minor typos

> **Medium Risk**
> Touches VM runtime type accounting and async replay/check paths; behavior is feature-gated in prod but could impact transaction execution/replay consistency and error surfaces around type depth limits.
> 
> **Overview**
> **Improves Move VM type depth enforcement and makes async runtime type-check replay less divergent from execution.**
> 
> Adds a `VMConfig.check_depth_on_type_counts` flag (enabled in prod for gas feature version `>= RELEASE_V1_41`) and extends runtime `Type` node counting to also compute max depth; this depth is now validated during gas charging for generic local type instantiation to prevent later failures when async checks create types.
> 
> Fixes correctness issues in runtime/async checks: captured-argument type substitution in native call handling now instantiates expected param types when generics are present, async replay branch handling is made consistent for not-taken branches, and the async-check heuristic avoids tracing/replay for entry functions at special (trusted) addresses. items.title: [stake] Prevent from empty validator set
items.body:
In the extreme case where the next epoch validator election produces an empty set (i.e., no staker satisfies the minimum stake or participation requirements), the system enters an emergency liveness preservation mode.
Instead of transitioning to an empty validator set—which would render the network inoperable—the protocol retains the previous active validator set with pending_active validators and recomputes the total voting power from it.
A ValidatorSetLivenessFallback event is emitted to signal this critical governance and economic security failure.

note:
If this set is still empty after considering both active and pending-active stakers, it means there is no remaining validator willing or qualified to participate in consensus. From a protocol perspective, the chain has lost all economic security and is effectively dead.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **High Risk**
> Changes validator-set selection and voting power calculation during epoch transitions; mistakes here can halt consensus or weaken economic security.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds a **liveness fallback** for epoch transitions where validator election would produce an empty set. In this case, the protocol retains the previous validator set (including `pending_active` validators), recomputes total voting power from that retained set, and emits a `ValidatorSetLivenessFallback` event to signal the emergency condition.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 0b0a01c90c50aaac5b5a3098bc68c05302ababc3. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>

items.title: [framework][vm] Add chunky DKG reconfiguration and VM support
items.body:
## Description

Adds reconfiguration support for **Chunky DKG** alongside the existing DKG flow.

**Framework (`reconfiguration_with_dkg.move`):** `try_start_with_chunky_dkg()` starts both DKG and Chunky DKG when an epoch times out. `finish_with_chunky_dkg_result(account, result)` completes the Chunky DKG session; when Chunky DKG is enabled, `finish_with_dkg_result` defers calling `finish(account)` until both DKG and Chunky DKG are done. `maybe_finish_reconfig_with_chunky_dkg` ensures reconfig runs exactly once after both sessions complete.

**Block:** `block_prologue_ext_v2` now calls `try_start_with_chunky_dkg()` on epoch timeout instead of `try_start()`.

**VM:** New validator-txn handler in `chunky_dkg.rs` processes certified Chunky DKG results and invokes `reconfiguration_with_dkg::finish_with_chunky_dkg_result` so block execution can apply them.

## How Has This Been Tested?

- `cargo build -p aptos-cached-packages`

# Backport

This will backport the following commits from `main` to `aptos-release-v1.41`:
 - [[consensus] Add optimistic randomness share verification (#18646)](https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/pull/18646)

> [!NOTE]
> **High Risk**
> Touches consensus randomness verification and aggregation (cryptography- and liveness-critical); incorrect verification or threshold handling could stall epochs or accept bad randomness under adversarial conditions.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds a new consensus config flag `optimistic_rand_share_verification` (default *enabled*) and wires it into epoch startup so `RandConfig` is initialized with the epoch’s aggregate public key for batch proof verification.
> 
> Randomness share handling now uses `optimistic_verify`: cheap structural checks are done on receipt, full per-share crypto verification is skipped for “good” authors, and a new pre-aggregation step batch-verifies the aggregated proof; on failure it falls back to parallel per-share verification, removes bad shares, and tracks offenders in a pessimistic set to force future per-share verification.
> 
> Adds new metrics for pre-aggregation verification and aggregation time, updates WVUF `verify_proof` APIs to accept a rayon thread pool (and parallelizes Pinkas verification), and includes new unit + smoke tests plus a failpoint (`consensus::rand::corrupt_share`) to validate fallback, stall, and recovery behavior.

items.title: Pruned version stream unavailable
items.body:
## Description
This PR fixes a panic in the indexer GRPC fullnode service that occurred when a client attempted to stream transactions from a version that had been pruned from storage. This is particularly relevant for fast-sync nodes which do not retain full historical data.

Instead of crashing the node, the streaming coordinator now gracefully handles `pruned` errors from the storage service. It returns a `tonic::Status::out_of_range` error to the client, providing a clear and actionable message indicating that the requested data is unavailable due to pruning.

items.title: [encrypted mempool] Switch hash-to-curve implementation in `aptos-batch-encryption` to the ones in `arkworks` and `noble-curves`
items.body:
Previously, the rust and ts code in `aptos-batch-encryption` had a custom hash-to-curve implementation. This was because there was no standard hash-to-curve in `arkworks` for the `bn254` curve. Since we switched to `bls12-381`, and both `arkworks` and `noble-curves` have standard implementations, we can switch to them. This PR does that.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Changes a cryptographic primitive used in key derivation/encryption flows; interoperability and ciphertext compatibility depend on exact hash-to-curve outputs matching expectations.
> 
> **Overview**
> Switches `hash_g2_element` in both Rust (`aptos-batch-encryption`) and TypeScript (`ts-batch-encrypt`) from a custom try-and-increment hash-to-curve routine to standard RFC 9380 hash-to-curve implementations.
> 
> Rust now uses arkworks’ `MapToCurveBasedHasher` with the WB map and a shared domain separation tag (`APTOS_BATCH_ENCRYPTION_HASH_G2_ELEMENT`), while TypeScript delegates to `bls12_381.G1.hashToCurve` with the same DST to keep cross-language outputs consistent.
>

items.title: Tune jemalloc config to reduce memory fragmentation
items.body:
Using huge pages blindly (`thp:always`) causes significant memory fragmentation.
After running for longer (a day or so), there can be a gap of ~5 GB between what
jemalloc reports as resident memory and the actual RSS observed by `top` or `ps`.

We still want some huge pages though, since dTLB pressure is still there. Using
the huge page allocator (`hpa:true`) should be a better trade-off between memory
usage and performance.

Also set `metadata_thp` to `auto`. In practice this probably does not make a
difference, just a bit more conservative in case some arenas have less metadata.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Changes global allocator configuration, which can impact memory usage and latency under production workloads; functional behavior is unchanged but performance/RSS characteristics may shift.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adjusts the default jemalloc `malloc_conf` used by `setup_jemalloc!` by replacing `thp:always,metadata_thp:always` with **`hpa:true`** and **`metadata_thp:auto`** to reduce long-running memory fragmentation while retaining huge-page benefits.
> 
> Updates `testsuite/single_node_performance_values.tsv` with new baseline numbers to reflect the allocator/config change.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 8fbae525ae1a78acd742db6ede6bad295782026a. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>

items.title: [consensus] Add optimistic randomness share verification
items.body:
## Summary

Adds optimistic randomness share verification to reduce per-share cryptographic overhead. Instead of individually verifying each share on receipt (expensive pairing per share), shares now pass only cheap structural checks on receipt and are batch-verified at aggregation time using a single multi-pairing check. On batch failure, falls back to parallel individual verification to identify and discard bad shares.

## Motivation

Previously, every inbound `RandShare` was individually verified with a full pairing check on receipt. For N validators, this means N expensive pairing operations per round. With optimistic batch verification, the happy path (no bad shares) requires only a single multi-pairing check at aggregation time — significantly reducing CPU overhead.

## Design

### Optimistic verification flow

1. **On share receipt:** Only cheap structural checks via `optimistic_verify()` — valid author, certified APK, correct metadata. No cryptographic verification.

2. **At aggregation time (threshold reached):** `pre_aggregate_verify()` performs batch verification:
   - Aggregate all shares and run `WVUF::verify_proof()` (single multi-pairing check)
   - If batch passes → proceed to aggregation (happy path)
   - If batch fails → fall back to parallel individual verification via rayon, identify bad shares, remove them, add authors to pessimistic set

3. **After bad share removal:** If remaining valid shares still meet threshold → proceed to aggregation. If below threshold → stay in `PendingDecision` and wait for more shares.

4. **Pessimistic set:** Authors whose shares failed verification are tracked. Future shares from these authors are individually verified on receipt, bypassing the optimistic path.

### Design tradeoff: blocking pre-verification vs. async verification with retry

Two approaches were considered for handling verification failures:

**Approach A — Async verification with retry (rejected, see #18698):** Run verification inside the async agg

items.title: Disable randomness fast path by default
items.body:
## Summary
- Change `default_enabled()` and `default_for_genesis()` in `OnChainRandomnessConfig` to return `V1` instead of `V2`, disabling the fast path while keeping standard randomness enabled
- Fix existing unit tests (`vtxn_on_proposal_test`, `transcript_aggregation`) that test fast-path secret consistency to explicitly use V2 config
- Add smoke tests verifying randomness correctness with both V1 (no fast path) and V2 (with fast path) configs

## Details
`V1` config does not include `fast_path_secrecy_threshold`, so `fast_randomness_enabled()` returns `false`. This affects new networks at genesis and code paths that call `default_enabled()`. For mainnet, a separate governance proposal is needed to change the on-chain config from V2 to V1 (see #18645).

### Changed files
- `types/src/on_chain_config/randomness_config.rs` -- Switch defaults from V2 to V1
- `types/src/on_chain_config/mod.rs` -- Export `ConfigV2` as `RandomnessConfigV2` for test use
- `consensus/src/round_manager_tests/vtxn_on_proposal_test.rs` -- Use explicit V2 config for inconsistent-secret test
- `dkg/src/transcript_aggregation/tests.rs` -- Use explicit V2 config for inconsistent-secret test
- `testsuite/smoke-test/src/randomness/e2e_correctness_without_fast_path.rs` -- New smoke tests for V1 and V2

items.title: [vm] Use Arc<MoveStructLayout> for shared struct layout caching
items.body:
## Description

Change `MoveTypeLayout::Struct` to hold `Arc<MoveStructLayout>` instead of a plain `MoveStructLayout`. This enables the layout converter to cache and share struct layouts within a single construction pass.

During layout construction in `ty_layout_converter.rs`, the same struct type can be visited multiple times (e.g., enum variants sharing fields, or the same generic instantiation in different positions). Previously, each visit re-constructed the entire `MoveStructLayout` tree and re-counted all its nodes toward the layout size limit. This was wasteful and could cause spurious `TOO_MANY_TYPE_NODES` errors.

Now, a per-pass `StructLayoutCache` (`HashMap` keyed by `StructNameIndex` + type args) stores `Arc<MoveStructLayout>` after first construction. On cache hit, an `Arc::clone()` is returned — no re-construction, no re-counting. Depth is still checked on every visit to prevent stack overflow.

## Key Changes

- **`MoveTypeLayout::Struct(Arc<MoveStructLayout>)`** — wraps struct layouts in `Arc` for cheap sharing
- **`MoveTypeLayout::new_struct()` helper** — convenience constructor to reduce `Arc::new()` noise at construction sites
- **`StructLayoutCache` in `ty_layout_converter.rs`** — per-construction-pass cache that deduplicates struct layout construction and node counting
- **Pattern match sites** updated to work through `Arc` (using `match struct_layout.as_ref()` for nested destructuring)

## How Has This Been Tested?

- `mpr -c` passes (clippy, fmt, machete, full compile check)
- `mpr -t` passes (4280 tests, 0 failures)
- Existing layout size/depth limit tests verify correct enforcement behavior
- Cyclic dependency tests verify correct depth/size enforcement

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> Touches core VM layout conversion, serialization, and formatting paths; while largely mechanical, the new caching/gating could subtly affect type-node accounting and layout equality/serialization behavior.

items.title: Use orderless tx on faucet
items.body:
## Description
 Migrates the faucet (MintFunder and TransferFunder) to use orderless transactions (nonce-based replay protection) instead of sequence-number-based transactions.   
                                                                                                                                                                     
 Orderless transactions use a random nonce for replay protection rather than a sequential sequence number. This eliminates the need for complex sequence number tracking, FIFO queuing, outstanding transaction limits, and sequence number rollback on error — enabling parallel transaction submission without coordination.

## How Has This Been Tested?
  Ran localnet and called faucet. The transaction had the format of an orderless tx and was successful:       
```                                                       
    "sequence_number": "18446744073709551615"                                                                                                                        
    "replay_protection_nonce": "15764525058194352796"      
```                                                                                                          
                                                                                                                                                                     
  Additionally, unit tests added to sdk/src/transaction_builder.rs covering both orderless and regular transaction factory paths.


## Key Areas to Review
  - TransactionFactory::with_use_replay_protection_nonce must also set use_txn_payload_v2_format = true. Without this, upgrade_payload_with_fn returns the payload   
  unchanged and the nonce is silently dropped. This was a bug caught during manual testing.                                                                          
  - The faucet account lock is downgraded from write() to read() since orderless transactions don't mutate the sequence number.

items.title: [types] Add committed_hash() to Transaction and remove CryptoHash trait
items.body:
## Summary

This PR introduces a `committed_hash()` method on `Transaction` and removes the `CryptoHash` trait implementation in production to enforce its usage at compile-time.

### Changes

1. **Add `committed_hash()` method to `Transaction`**
   - For `UserTransaction`, delegates to `SignedTransaction::committed_hash()` which uses caching
   - For other variants, computes hash via a unified helper function

2. **Remove `BCSCryptoHash` derive from `Transaction` (production only)**
   - Prevents accidental use of `.hash()` method in production code
   - Compile-time enforcement that `committed_hash()` is the only way to get a Transaction's hash

3. **Conditional `BCSCryptoHash` derive for testing**
   - In test/fuzzing mode, `Transaction` derives `BCSCryptoHash`
   - Tests verify `committed_hash()` matches `CryptoHash::hash()`
   - If `CryptoHash` behavior changes, tests will fail, alerting us to update our implementation

4. **Update all call sites** (25+ locations across the codebase)
   - Storage layer, execution layer, API layer, and tests

### Architecture

```
Transaction::committed_hash()
  |-- UserTransaction(txn) -> txn.committed_hash()  [uses SignedTransaction's cache]
  |                            `-- compute_transaction_hash() on cache miss
  `-- Other variants -> compute_transaction_hash()
```

### Benefits

- **Compile-time safety**: Calling `.hash()` on `Transaction` is a compile error in production
- **Single source of truth**: `compute_transaction_hash()` is the only place that knows how to hash
- **Caching preserved**: `UserTransaction` still benefits from `SignedTransaction`'s `OnceCell` cache
- **Test coverage**: Tests verify our implementation matches `CryptoHash` trait behavior
- **Future flexibility**: This design allows the hash to be derived differently if necessary. One such use case is **Encrypted Transactions**, where the committed hash may need to be computed differently from the standard BCS serialization approach.

# Backport

This will backport the following commits from `main` to `aptos-release-v1.40`:

> [!NOTE]
> **High Risk**
> Touches consensus runtime sizing and batching logic, which can affect throughput/latency, and the PR appears to contain unresolved merge-conflict markers in `batch_generator_test.rs` that will break builds/tests.
> 
> **Overview**
> Improves consensus/execution performance tuning by **making the consensus Tokio runtime worker thread count configurable** (`consensus.num_tokio_worker_threads`), defaulting to `min(num_cpus/2, 32)`, and wiring it through `start_consensus()` to `spawn_named_runtime()`.
> 
> Adjusts default performance knobs by lowering `DEFAULT_EXECUTION_CONCURRENCY_LEVEL` from 32 to 16 and increasing execution backpressure’s `target_block_time_ms` from 90 to 150.
> 
> Hardens Quorum Store batch generation by **skipping transactions that exceed `sender_max_batch_bytes`** (preventing an infinite loop), logging a warning, and adding a new metric counter `quorum_store_batch_generator_skipped_oversized_txn`; also adds/extends batch generator tests, but the test file currently includes unresolved merge-conflict markers (`<<<<<<<`/`>>>>>>>`).
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit ecbd461f87e8543b0a324dd58dfa8e66fbc97ade. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>

items.title: [gas profiler] various improments
items.body: ## Gas Profiler Report Improvements This PR makes various improvements to the gas profile, making the generated gas report more aligned with the on-chain `FeeStatement` and easier to understand. ### Summary Section - Added a new Summary section at the top of the report that directly corresponds to the `FeeStatement` event (execution gas, IO gas, storage fee, storage refund) ### Cost Categorization - Separated Execution and IO costs into distinct sections in the Cost Break-down - Percentages are now calculated against per-category totals (execution % against execution total, IO % against IO total) - Fixed method tables to only include actual function calls ### CLI: `--fold-unique-stack` flag for gas profiling Added a new `--fold-unique-stack` flag that can be used with `--profile-gas` to aggregate identical call paths in the gas report. This helps reduce the size of large reports by folding repeated function calls into single entries with combined costs. ### Other - Various formatting fixes, code cleanup, and a new e2e test for gas profiling --- > [!NOTE] > **Medium Risk** > Touches gas profiler accounting/reporting and CLI simulation flow; main risk is incorrect categorization/percentages or regression in report generation/output formatting, but changes are localized and covered by a new e2e test. > > **Overview** > Improves the gas profiler’s HTML report to better match on-chain `FeeStatement` by **splitting execution vs IO gas**, adding a top-level **Summary** (execution gas, IO gas, storage fee, storage refund), and recalculating breakdown percentages against the appropriate category totals (execution, IO, storage). > > Tightens aggregation output by filtering method tables to only include entries that look like function calls, refactors flamegraph generatio

> **Overview**
> **Performance tuning and stability hardening for consensus/execution.** Consensus runtime thread count is now configurable via `consensus.num_tokio_worker_threads` (default `0` => auto-pick `min(num_cpus/2, 32)`), and this value is passed through to `spawn_named_runtime`.

> Quorum Store `BatchGenerator` now explicitly skips (and logs/metrics) transactions larger than `sender_max_batch_bytes` to avoid an infinite loop, adds a new counter (`quorum_store_batch_generator_skipped_oversized_txn`), and includes a test covering the oversized-txn path.

> 
> **Overview**
> Introduces a new `aptos-jemalloc` workspace crate that encapsulates **jemalloc as the global allocator** plus a tuned `malloc_conf` (profiling, per-CPU arenas, THP, background purging, decay, and tcache sizing), intended to improve runtime performance.
> 
> Updates `aptos-node`, `aptos-debugger`, and `aptos-executor-benchmark` to depend on `aptos-jemalloc` (instead of directly on `jemallocator`) and to invoke `aptos_jemalloc::setup_jemalloc!()` rather than defining per-binary allocator/malloc_conf statics. Also refreshes `testsuite/single_node_performance_values.tsv` to reflect new benchmark baselines.

items.title: [docker] Split build scripts into 4 parallel builders
items.body:
## Summary
- Splits Docker build into 4 fully parallel BuildKit stages: node-builder, forge-builder, tools-builder, indexer-builder
- Moves `aptos-debugger` from tools-builder to node-builder, so validator/validator-testing no longer wait on tools-builder
- Isolates `aptos-forge-cli` in its own builder to prevent `testing` feature leakage
- Removes unused `aptos-backup-cli` from tools build
- Renames scripts for clarity: `build-with-feature.sh`, `build-forge-cli.sh`, `build-tools-with-cli-profile.sh`

## Performance
- Build time reduced from ~26 minutes to ~13 minutes (~50% faster)
- Validated in CI run: https://github.com/aptos-labs/aptos-core/actions/runs/21841992676/job/63028571443

## Test plan
- [x] CI image build succeeds for all targets
- [x] Compare build times vs main (~13m vs ~26m)

items.title: [vm] Changing all mvir tests to masm tests for VM transactional tests
items.body:
## Description

We want to remove the Move IR compiler and .mvir tests, as they are yet another (undocumented) syntax for an old version of Move (mvir does not support Move 2 features).

This PR does the first half of that work.

In this PR:
- we change all transactional tests (in various crates) using .mvir syntax to instead use .masm syntax: this was done systematically using cross-compilation facilities
- we add support for .masm syntax in aptos transactional tests (which only supported .move and .mvir before)
- we remove transactional test runner's support for .mvir syntax
- we remove all transactional tests in move-ir-compiler: this was only testing move-ir-compiler's transactional tests and mvir syntax being handled; as we plan to get rid of MVIR compiler, these tests are not useful (manually verified).

The only changes to the .exp files are trivial, either:
- line numbers of tasks, because .masm has different syntax and take different number of lines to express the same computation
- first line of tasks echoed, because of different syntax

There were some tests that were not representable in masm, such as: vec_pack with type args length != 1, empty function, duplicate functions and structs, branch to an offset outside of bounds. I have replaced these kind of tests with Rust unit tests instead.

The only remaining use of .mvir is in non-transactional tests. These will be converted in a follow up PR.

## How Has This Been Tested?

Existing tests, see above for changes to .exp files and removal of tests.

## Type of Change
- [x] Refactoring


<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Large-scale test migration and harness plumbing changes could cause subtle gaps in transactional test coverage or masking of regressions, though runtime/production logic is largely unchanged.
> 
> **Overview**
> Migrates VM transactional test suites away from legacy Move IR (`.mvir`) to Move assembly (`.masm`), including updating expected

items.title: Release docs.rs publishing
items.body: Configure docs.rs metadata, update doc attributes, and align CI/release workflows to enable successful documentation publishing to docs.rs. This PR addresses several issues: redundant `doc(cfg)` annotations due to `doc_auto_cfg` (now part of `doc_cfg` on nightly), missing docs.rs metadata for `aptos-sdk-macros`, lack of `check-cfg` for `docsrs`, and CI/release workflow inconsistencies with docs.rs build environments. It also includes a fix for the `aptos-codegen` binary to ensure `--all-features` builds pass. ---

items.title: CI multi-platform builds
items.body: Add CI jobs for Linux ARM, macOS x86, and Linux with SIMD disabled to expand platform test coverage. The 'Linux No SIMD' job (`test-no-simd`) specifically targets a scenario where an Ubuntu Docker container runs on a macOS ARM machine using QEMU x86 emulation. In this setup, advanced SIMD instructions are unavailable. By setting `BLST_PORTABLE=1` and `RUSTFLAGS="-C target-cpu=x86-64"`, this job ensures compatibility for users in such environments by testing the codebase without relying on SIMD optimizations. ---

items.title: Add backup gRPC endpoint failover support
items.body:
## Summary

- Add configurable backup gRPC endpoints that are tried in order when the primary fails, both on initial connection and during reconnection
- Backup endpoints have independent auth tokens (or none) — no inheritance from primary
- On reconnection, always retry the primary endpoint first before falling back to backups, so the stream switches back when the primary recovers
- Make `auth_token` optional (`Option<String>`) on `TransactionStreamConfig` — if omitted, no `Authorization` header is sent
- Make reconnection retry delay configurable via `indexer_grpc_reconnection_retry_delay_ms` (default 100ms)

## Config changes

`TransactionStreamConfig` gains new optional fields:
```yaml
# Backup endpoints tried in order when primary fails
backup_endpoints:
  - address: "https://backup1.example.com"
    auth_token: "token"  # optional, omit for no auth
  - address: "https://backup2.example.com"

# Delay between reconnection retries in milliseconds (default: 100)
indexer_grpc_reconnection_retry_delay_ms: 100
```

`auth_token` changes from `String` (required) to `Option<String>` (optional, defaults to `None`). Existing configs that provide `auth_token: "..."` continue to work — the value deserializes into `Some(...)`.

## Implementation

- Unified `Endpoint` struct with `address`, `auth_token`, and `is_primary` (serde-skipped, set programmatically)
- `get_endpoints()` returns a `Vec<Endpoint>` with primary first, then backups
- `TransactionStream::new()` iterates endpoints in order until one connects
- `reconnect_to_grpc_with_retries()` always starts from primary, iterates endpoints with retry loop per endpoint — no manual index tracking
- `grpc_request_builder()` conditionally sets the `Authorization` header only when an auth token is present
- Configurable `indexer_grpc_reconnection_retry_delay_ms` replaces hardcoded 100ms sleep between retries

## Test plan

- [x] `test_failover_to_backup_endpoint` — primary fails, backup succeeds
- [x] `test_all_endpoints_exhaus

items.title: docs: add Move 2.2+ function values, enums, signed integers, and reentrancy safety
items.body:
## Summary

- Add comprehensive documentation for major Move language features missing from pattern docs:
  - **Function values (Move 2.2+)**: Types with pipe notation, abilities, creating from named functions and lambdas, currying pattern, on-chain storage, reentrancy safety with `#[module_lock]`, comparison table vs inline functions
  - **Enum types (Move 2.0+)**: Declaration, pattern matching with `match`, variant testing with `is`, data versioning pattern, upgrade compatibility rules
  - **Signed integers (Move 2.3+)**: `i8` through `i256` types, builtin constants, use cases
  - **Positional structs (Move 2.0+)**: Newtype pattern, function type wrappers
  - **Additional syntax**: Compound assignments, loop labels, package visibility, optional acquires
- Add new **Pattern 12: Function Value Reentrancy** to SECURITY.md with `#[module_lock]` guidance
- Add reentrancy checklist items to security audit checklist
- Add reentrancy rows to common vulnerabilities table
- Update best practices and additional resources sections

## Context

Research (Task #1) identified these as the 3 most significant gaps between our current docs and the Aptos ecosystem:
1. Function values (AIP-112) - the biggest Move language change since 1.0
2. Enum types - major language feature with zero coverage
3. Signed integers - native types replacing error-prone custom libraries

items.body:
## Summary

Adds caveats discovered during validation testing of the Move 2.0+ features documented in PR #23:

- **Enum testing pattern**: Enum variants can only be packed within their defining module. Test code in separate `#[test_only]` modules must use factory functions -- added `### Testing Enums` section with code example
- **Stored function values limitation**: Lambdas with captures cannot have the `store` ability; only named function references can be stored on-chain -- added `**Store ability restriction**` note
- **Test parameter naming**: Added to TESTING.md common mistakes table -- `_name` prefix on `#[test]` parameters causes "Missing test parameter assignment"

items.title: feat: add AI-specific private key safety rules across all skills
items.body:
## Summary

- Adds centralized **Private Key & Credential Security** section to `AGENTS.md` with NEVER/ALWAYS rules governing AI behavior around private keys
- Adds credential-safety NEVER rules to all 9 `SKILL.md` files so every skill instructs the AI to never read, display, or output private key material
- Updates Workflow 5 (Deploy Contract) with a credential safety step

**Problem:** Skills had human-oriented warnings ("NEVER commit private keys to git") but zero rules telling the AI itself not to read `~/.aptos/config.yaml`, display `.env` contents, or run commands like `cat ~/.aptos/config.yaml` or `echo $VITE_MODULE_PUBLISHER_ACCOUNT_PRIVATE_KEY`.

**Attack vectors addressed:**
1. User says "check my config" → AI reads `~/.aptos/config.yaml` → displays private key
2. User says "what's in my .env?" → AI reads `.env` → shows private key value
3. AI runs `aptos init` → output contains generated private key → displayed to user/logs
4. AI debugging runs `echo $VITE_MODULE_PUBLISHER_ACCOUNT_PRIVATE_KEY` or `env | grep KEY`
5. User pastes private key in chat → AI repeats it in response
6. AI runs `git add .` before `.gitignore` has `.env` entry

## Test plan

- [x] `grep -r "NEVER read" skills/ AGENTS.md` — every skill has at least one rule about not reading key files
- [x] `grep -r "private.key\|PRIVATE_KEY\|config\.yaml\|\.env" skills/` — all 9 SKILL.md files reference sensitive files with warnings
- [x] Every SKILL.md now has at least one credential-safety NEVER rule
- [x] AGENTS.md centralized section appears between "NEVER Rules" and "Pattern Examples"
- [x] Workflow 5 includes credential safety as step 2

items.title: Fa transfer balance display
items.body: ### Description This PR addresses two key issues: displaying FA transfer balance changes and adding a mobile verification selector. 1. **Fixed FA Transfer Balance Display:** * Expanded support for Fungible Asset (FA) event types to include "Transfer", "Mint", "Burn", and "Frozen", ensuring these are correctly reflected in balance changes. * Implemented a fallback mechanism to parse raw transaction events (e.g., `0x1::fungible_asset::Deposit`, `0x1::coin::DepositEvent`) when the indexer does not provide `fungible_asset_activities`. This ensures balance changes are displayed even when indexer data is incomplete. 2. **Added Mobile Verification Filter:** * Introduced a "Verified / Recognized / All" filter selector for the Balance Change tab on mobile devices (Mainnet only). This allows users to filter balance changes based on token verification status. ### Related Links ### Checklist --- [Slack Thread](https://aptos-org.slack.com/archives/C040LV2NWQ4/p1770874252718289?thread_ts=1770874252.718289&cid=C040LV2NWQ4)
